Potatoes.heritage_note: First early potato popular in Ireland since the 1940s; prized for its delicate skin and quick cooking.

Oats.heritage_note: A resilient landrace suited to Atlantic winds; central to traditional porridge and bannocks.

Carrageen Moss.heritage_note: For generations, carrageen has thickened puddings and broths along the coast.

Cabbage.heritage_note: Victorian-era cultivar cherished in winter stews across Irish homes.
